name: Camino de las pilas de Trisla — circular desde Jimena
region_intro: Jogging routes around Jimena de la Frontera traverse a diverse landscape, from the mountainous terrain of Sierra Mágina Natural Park to the serene paths of the Canavan Pine Forest. The region is characterized by varied inclines and declines, offering both challenging trail running and more leisurely jogs through olive and fig groves. Runners can also explore rugged river valleys like the Hozgarganta, featuring rock formations and wild flora. This geological and botanical diversity provides a range of running routes catering to different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Jimena The most popular jogging route is Ascent to Pico de las Cuevas del Aire — circular from Bedmar, a difficult 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Jimena y Cueva de la Graja — circular, a moderate 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This shorter route provides a scenic jog near the town, featuring moderate elevation changes. Local runners also love the Torreón de Cuadros and Cueva del Agua Río Cuadras by the sendero de las Viñas — circular by the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Jogging around Jimena is defined by mountainous natural parks, unique pine forests, and tranquil agricultural expanses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to challenging trail runs. The routes in Jimena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Jimena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Jimena de la Frontera?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Jimena de la Frontera, offering a diverse range of experiences from easy jogs to challenging trail runs. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 150 runners using them.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Jimena?
A: Yes, while many routes offer a challenge, there is at least one easy jogging route available. For a moderate option that's still manageable for many, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362897710/jimena-y-cueva-de-la-graja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jimena y Cueva de la Graja — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.9 miles (4.6 km) long and features moderate elevation changes near the town.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: The terrain around Jimena de la Frontera is incredibly varied. You'll find mountainous paths within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park, serene trails through cork oak forests, and rugged river valleys like the Hozgarganta. The village itself offers narrow, steep, cobbled streets for a unique urban running experience. Expect a mix of inclines and declines, providing both challenging trail running and more leisurely jogs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Jimena de la Frontera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362897710/jimena-y-cueva-de-la-graja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jimena y Cueva de la Graja — circular&lt;/a&gt;, or for a more challenging option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362871797/ascent-to-pico-de-las-cuevas-del-aire-circular-from-bedmar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico de las Cuevas del Aire — circular from Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while running?
A: Jimena de la Frontera offers stunning panoramic views from its elevated position, including glimpses of Gibraltar and Algeciras on clear days. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers incredible vistas. You can also explore the picturesque Hozgarganta River, known for its waterfalls and tranquil Andalusian countryside, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I incorporate historical sites into my running routes?
A: Absolutely! The village is overshadowed by an 8th-century Moorish and Roman castle, a national monument that offers incredible views. Running through the narrow, steep streets of Jimena de la Frontera itself, part of Andalusia's 'Pueblos Blancos,' is also a historical experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Cuadros&lt;/a&gt; is another historical landmark you might encounter on longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362892094/torreon-de-cuadros-and-cueva-del-agua-rio-cuadras-by-the-sendero-de-las-vinas-circular-by-the-parque-natural-de-sierra-magina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Cuadros and Cueva del Agua Río Cuadras by the sendero de las Viñas — circular by the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: The running routes in Jimena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from mountainous natural parks and unique cork oak forests to tranquil agricultural expanses, and the blend of challenging trails with scenic beauty and historical intrigue.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options near Jimena?
A: Yes, Jimena de la Frontera is located on the impressive GR 7 long-distance hiking route, which traverses Spain. This offers extensive options for those looking for longer runs or multi-day trail running adventures. Additionally, routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362892094/torreon-de-cuadros-and-cueva-del-agua-rio-cuadras-by-the-sendero-de-las-vinas-circular-by-the-parque-natural-de-sierra-magina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Cuadros and Cueva del Agua Río Cuadras by the sendero de las Viñas — circular by the Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina&lt;/a&gt; cover over 10 miles (16 km).

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: Jimena de la Frontera is suitable for running year-round, but the warmer months offer the added benefit of being able to cool off in safe swimming spots along the Hozgarganta River. The varied landscape, including dense cork oak forests, provides shade during hotter periods, while the mild winters make it an attractive destination for off-season running.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by rivers or offer opportunities to cool off?
A: Yes, the Hozgarganta River borders the village to its west, and a popular trail runs along it. The river's clean water provides opportunities for cooling off in safe swimming spots during warmer months, making it a refreshing feature for runners.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging running trails for advanced runners?
A: For advanced runners seeking a challenge, Jimena offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362871797/ascent-to-pico-de-las-cuevas-del-aire-circular-from-bedmar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico de las Cuevas del Aire — circular from Bedmar&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3-mile (13.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362906729/camino-de-las-pilas-de-trisla-circular-desde-jimena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de las pilas de Trisla — circular desde Jimena&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option covering over 7.5 miles (12 km).
tips: Jimena is a small town in the south of the province, on the northern side of the Sierra Mágina, located in a landscape of olive groves, fruit trees, allotments and sierra. The town is particularly known for its figs and brebas. The municipality is divided into two very different areas: there is the mountainous south-eastern area that is part of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, and the rest of the municipality is agricultural land dedicated to olives.
At this point there is the break that goes in the direction of the Cueva de la Graja, a place that had been inhabited since ancient times and where a group of cave paintings can be found.

Beautiful path that is located in the old middle of Pinar de Cánava and where the Caracoles are found, a set of small stone cabins in the middle of this valley surrounded by high mountains that show us how far away life must have been in these Andalusian areas.
